description.en: Batch data from the iterable into tuples of length n. The last batch may be shorter than n. If strict is true, will raise a ValueError if the final batch is shorter than n. Loops over the input iterable and accumulates data into tuples up to size n. The input is consumed lazily, just enough to fill a batch. The result is yielded as soon as the batch is full or when the input iterable is exhausted: >>> flattened_data = ['roses', 'red', 'violets', 'blue', 'sugar', 'sweet'] >>> unflattened = lis...
